message:
test(unistd): serialize gai_*/getaddrinfo_a tests with GAI_TEST_LOCK (bd-el0v8)

`synchronous_gai_wrappers_match_host_degenerate_contracts` and 15
sibling getaddrinfo_a / gai_cancel / gai_error / gai_suspend tests
all compare host glibc behavior against our ABI shim using a
capture-host / capture-abi / assert-equal pattern. Host glibc
maintains a single in-process async-getaddrinfo request queue + a
global state cache — concurrent host gai_* calls in parallel test
threads can mutate that state between our captured host snapshot
and the matching abi snapshot, breaking the parity assertions.

Reproduced ~25% of stress runs at --test-threads=16 in the prior
bd-el0v8 sweep. Errno is thread-local so the singletest in
isolation passes 10/10, but two parallel test threads in the same
binary race on the host's global queue and produce divergent
errno-side observations.

Add `static GAI_TEST_LOCK: Mutex<()>` and acquire it as the first
line of all 16 gai-related tests. Within-binary serialization is
sufficient because cargo test runs each test binary in a separate
process; host glibc's queue state is per-process.

Verified: 14/14 gai-* tests pass single-pass after the lock. Full
8-iteration --test-threads=16 stress validation in flight.
